problem text: A spirit rolls a fair die with $6$ faces. You have the option to wager on whether the result is even or odd. For each $4$ wagered on an even outcome, you will get back $6$ if your prediction is correct. Likewise, for each $6$ wagered on an odd result, you will receive $9$ if you are right. To play with the best strategy, how much should you stake on this game?
problem solution: Assume we place a bet of $x$ units on an even result and $y$ on an odd result. Thus, the total amount wagered is $4x+6y$. The expected gain from this game is $0.5 \cdot 6x + 0.5 \cdot 9y = 3x + 4.5y = \frac{3}{4} \cdot (4x + 6y)$. Consequently, our anticipated return is $\frac{3}{4}$ of our wager, indicating that we should not engage in this game as the expected value is 0.
problem hint: Calculate the expected value when $x$ units are wagered on an even outcome and $y$ on an odd outcome.
